calendarNotes:
San Diego Unified begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 10, 2026 — the same start date as LAUSD, and about two weeks earlier than Long Beach Unified (August 25). Spring Recess runs March 29–April 2, 2027, returning students to class on April 5. The school year ends May 26, 2027. San Diego's mild, marine climate means weather-related cancellations are exceptionally rare — once the official calendar is published, families can plan around it with high confidence.

San Diego Unified serves one of the highest concentrations of military families of any large urban school district in the country, with major installations including Camp Pendleton, Naval Air Station North Island, Marine Corps Air Station Miramar, and Naval Base San Diego all within the metro area. Mid-year enrollment transfers are significantly more common in SDUSD than in most California districts — if your family is relocating to or from a military installation, contact the district's Military Family Liaison to expedite paperwork and school placement.

San Diego's border location creates a unique dynamic for families with ties to Tijuana and northern Baja California. Mexican public schools follow a September-to-June academic calendar with a two-week spring break in mid-April — roughly two weeks later than SDUSD's spring recess. Cross-border families should verify both calendars before booking travel, as the two systems rarely align on break dates.

Like all California public schools, SDUSD observes Cesar Chavez Day (March 31) as a school holiday. In the 2026-2027 school year, March 31 falls during spring recess, so families may not notice the separation. In years when spring break does not cover March 31, this California-specific holiday can catch families from other states off guard.
